name: Stewed Lamb Shanks & White Beans
recipeIngredient: 8 Each Lamb Shanks, About 1 Lb Each, bones cracked
28 Ounces Can Whole Tomatoes In Puree
recipeInstructions:
1. Soak the beans overnight in water to cover and drain or use the "quick soak" method.
2. Season lamb shanks with 1 teaspoon of the salt and pepper to taste.
3. Heat olive oil over medium-high heat in a large deep skillet.
4. Add the lamb shanks on all sides, about 10 minutes (do not crowd; work in batches if needed, removing fat as necessary).
5. Remove browned shanks to a platter. Add garlic, carrots, onions, and celery to the skillet and cook until softened, about 10 minutes.
6. Pour in the wine and cook for 2 minutes. Transfer mixture to a large stockpot.
7. Add tomatoes, breaking them up with the back of a spoon. Add beans, shanks, stock, rosemary, and bay leaves. Bring to boil, then reduce to a simmer and skim surface as needed.
8. Cover and cook until lamb and beans are tender, about 2 hours.
9. Skim as much fat from the surface as possible. Remove shanks using tongs to individual plates.
10. Season bean mixture with salt and pepper if needed. Discard rosemary and bay leaves. Using a slotted spoon, arrange some of the bean mixture around each lamb shank. Serve immediately.
